title: Telegram Integration
summary: Complete guide to Telegram bot integration including BotFather setup, privacy mode, webhook vs polling, group forum topics, per-chat sessions, streaming via draft/edit transport, slash command ACL, reactions, per-channel prompts, and DNS fallback configuration.
key_concepts: BotFather bot creation and token acquisition
Privacy mode: disable for group message access
Private chat topics for multi-session DMs
Slash command ACL per-user and per-group
DNS fallback IPs for restricted regions
Table rendering via markdown or HTML parse mode

env_vars.name: TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL
env_vars.description: Public HTTPS URL for webhook mode; if unset, uses long-polling

env_vars.name: TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET
env_vars.description: Secret token sent in X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header for webhook verification

env_vars.name: TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHATS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated list of allowed chat IDs or usernames; gateway denies all others

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Telegram listener
Create a named private chat topic for a separate DM session
List all active private chat topics
Delete a private chat topic and its session

config_keys.description: Message parse mode: Markdown or HTML (default: Markdown)
Enable streaming via draft/edit transport (default: true)
Send reaction emoji as response acknowledgment
Map of chat_id to system prompt override
Fallback IPs for api.telegram.org in restricted regions

slash_commands.description: Manage named DM sub-sessions (create, list, delete)
Show available commands for the bot

code_examples.title: Minimal Telegram config
code_examples.code:
telegram:
  token: ""
  allowed_chats:
    - "@myusername"
    - "-1001234567890"  # group chat ID

code_examples.title: Per-channel prompt override
code_examples.code:
telegram:
  per_channel_prompts:
    "-1001234567890": "You are a support bot for AcmeCorp. Only answer questions about our products."
    "@myusername": "You are my personal assistant."

code_examples.title: Create a named DM topic
code_examples.code:
/topic work
# Creates a separate session called "work" in your DM with the bot
/topic list
# Lists: default, work
/topic delete work

gotchas: Privacy mode must be disabled via BotFather (/setprivacy -> Disable) or the bot will not receive group messages
Bot must be added as admin to supergroups to read messages and use forum topics
Webhook mode requires a valid publicly accessible HTTPS URL; self-signed certs not accepted by Telegram
Long-polling and webhook cannot run simultaneously; only one mode active at a time
Forum topics require the supergroup to have topics enabled in Telegram group settings
Streaming uses draft-then-edit pattern: bot sends a draft, then edits it as tokens arrive; this counts against Telegram edit rate limits
Telegram limits bot message edits to ~20/minute per chat; high-throughput streams may hit this
HTML parse_mode requires proper entity escaping; Markdown mode has its own escape rules for special chars

409 Conflict cycle: 'terminated by other getUpdates request' errors for ~30s after killing the previous gateway process — Telegram's long-poll connection takes that long to expire server-side (#18513). Either wait or restart slowly
DeepSeek v4 Pro via OpenRouter sends Hermes gateway into a crash loop, freezing the Telegram bot (#16677) — avoid as default model for the Telegram gateway

Group-chat context-compression deadlock: gateway becomes unresponsive after compression is exhausted; session stays oversized; compression fail loop repeats (#9893). Recovery: session reset, not retry

Combined failure mode: `/cc` (session_reset) arriving while a thread has a running agent + credential pool exhausted = thread permanently stops receiving messages (#28686). Rotate keys BEFORE pool exhaustion or reset thread manually
full_text:
## Telegram

Connect Hermes to Telegram via the official Bot API.

### Setup

1. Message [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) and run .
2. Copy the token it returns — this is your .
3. Run  → select your bot → **Disable** so the bot can read group messages.

### Minimal config

```yaml
telegram:
  token: ""
  allowed_chats:
    - "@myusername"
    - "-1001234567890"  # group chat ID
```

Start the gateway:

```
hermes gateway
```

### Webhook mode

By default Hermes uses long-polling. To switch to webhooks set  to a publicly reachable HTTPS endpoint:

```yaml
telegram:
  webhook_url: "https://my-server.example.com/telegram/webhook"
  webhook_secret: ""
```

Telegram will POST updates to that URL with the secret token in .

### Private chat topics ()

In a 1-on-1 DM you can run multiple isolated sessions using named topics:

```
/topic work
```

Creates a new session called *work*. Future messages in this DM go to the *work* session until you switch back () or delete it ().

Use  to see all active topics.

### Group forum topics

When a supergroup has **Topics** enabled, each forum topic gets its own Hermes session automatically. No config needed — session IDs are derived from the topic thread ID.

### Streaming transport

Hermes sends a placeholder message, then edits it repeatedly as tokens stream in. This gives a real-time typing feel inside Telegram. To disable:

```yaml
telegram:
  stream: false
```

Note: Telegram limits edits to ~20/minute per chat. Streaming long responses in busy groups may trigger rate limits.

### Parse mode

Default is . Switch to  if your prompts generate HTML-tagged output:

```yaml
telegram:
  parse_mode: HTML
```

### Per-channel prompts

Override the system prompt for specific chats:

```yaml
telegram:
  per_channel_prompts:
    "-1001234567890": "You are a support bot for AcmeCorp."
    "@myusername": "You are my personal assistant."
```

### Group allowlisting

 accepts chat IDs (integers) or usernames (strings with ). The gateway rejects messages from any chat not on the list. Leave empty to allow all (not recommended for public bots).

### Slash command ACL

Restrict slash commands to specific users or groups:

```yaml
telegram:
  command_acl:
    admin_users:
      - "@myusername"
    admin_chats:
      - "-1001234567890"
```

### Reactions

Enable emoji reactions as a lightweight acknowledgment:

```yaml
telegram:
  reactions: true
```

The bot will react with 👍 when it receives a message and 🤖 when it finishes responding.

### DNS fallback

For regions where  is blocked, provide fallback IPs:

```yaml
telegram:
  dns_fallback_ips:
    - "149.154.167.220"
    - "149.154.167.221"
    - "91.108.4.14"
```

Hermes will attempt these IPs directly if DNS resolution fails.
